name: Sunrow Wheat
description: A bright-headed grain that leans with the meadow wind in tidy rows. Millers sort each bundle by stalk height before it reaches the stones.

name: Ditchmint
description: Cool green sprigs that crowd the irrigation ditches. Sheep avoid it until tea is poured, then become extremely interested.

name: Speckled Turnip
description: A round meadow turnip with freckles on its purple crown. Every root is just different enough to ruin a perfectly neat basket.

name: Boundary Bloom
description: A rare iris whose petals sit between the meadow's familiar families. Collectors debate its place; the flower seems content to be difficult.

name: Mill-Stone Grit
description: Sparkling dust swept from Bootstrap's millstones after a long day's turning. A pinch gives crafted tools a steady, trustworthy weight.

name: Softspun Wool
description: Cloud-soft wool from the meadow flocks, washed in ditch water and combed into even locks. It still smells faintly of sunshine and sheep opinions.

name: Millpond Scale
description: A silver-green scale found along the millpond bank. Its rings record a fish's seasons so neatly that patient anglers compare them by lanternlight.

name: Ditchbed Clay
description: Smooth blue-gray clay from the quiet bends of irrigation ditches. It holds a thumbprint so faithfully that potters use scraps for counting marks.

name: Honeyed Turnip Bites
description: Roasted turnip pieces glazed with meadow honey. Sweet, earthy, and much less argumentative than a raw turnip, they restore one heart.

name: Ditchmint Tea
description: A cooling cup brewed from ditchmint and a little honey. Clears trail dust from the throat and restores one and a half hearts.

name: Sunrow Biscuit
description: A honey-brushed wheat biscuit baked firm enough for a long walk. It crunches loudly enough to alert nearby geese and restores one heart.

name: Boundary Bloom Cordial
description: A shimmering cordial made from one boundary bloom and common iris petals. Its flavor refuses every tidy description while restoring four hearts.

name: Meadow Measure Cord
description: A knotted cord for comparing stalks, petals, and the occasional heroic turnip. Its weighted end keeps even windy measurements honest.

name: Boundary Stone Rubbing
description: A careful rubbing of the Old Boundary Stones' looping marks. Turn it sideways and the same pattern seems to choose a different path.

name: Whispering Well Pebble
description: A smooth pebble from beside the Whispering Well, warm only when held with others like it. Bit calls the quiet hum singing; the pebble declines to comment.
